scenario: Enterprise GenAI assistant on Microsoft estate
useWhenTrigger: Microsoft identity, M365, Defender or Fabric are strategic
azureOption: Microsoft Foundry / Azure OpenAI / Microsoft 365 Copilot
chooseAwsWhen: Choose AWS for broad model choice and AWS-native systems.
chooseAzureWhen: Choose Azure for Microsoft identity, security, productivity and data integration.
chooseGoogleCloudWhen: Choose GCP for Gemini, multimodal AI and BigQuery/Vertex integration.
complexityCaution: Moderate — Confirm data residency, model availability, content safety and licensing.

useWhenTrigger: You want managed access to many first- and third-party models
chooseAwsWhen: Strong breadth and mature Bedrock integration.
chooseAzureWhen: Strong enterprise controls and Microsoft ecosystem integration.
chooseGoogleCloudWhen: Strong Google models, AI infrastructure and data integration.
complexityCaution: Moderate — Benchmark model quality, latency and token economics for the actual use case.

useWhenTrigger: Answers must be grounded in governed enterprise content
awsOption: Knowledge Bases for Amazon Bedrock / Amazon Kendra
googleCloudOption: Vertex AI Search / Vertex AI RAG Engine
chooseAwsWhen: Strong when content and apps already run on AWS.
chooseAzureWhen: Strong for Microsoft 365, Purview and Azure estates.
chooseGoogleCloudWhen: Strong for BigQuery, Google Workspace and Vertex AI estates.
complexityCaution: Moderate — Evaluate retrieval quality, ACL propagation, freshness and citation support.

useWhenTrigger: Agents must use tools, memory, workflows and enterprise controls
chooseAwsWhen: Best for AWS-native tools and operational services.
chooseAzureWhen: Best for Microsoft enterprise integration and governance.
chooseGoogleCloudWhen: Best for Gemini, ADK, data and cloud-native agent engineering.
complexityCaution: Advanced — Start with one bounded workflow before multi-agent orchestration.

useWhenTrigger: Teams need training, registry, pipelines, deployment and monitoring
chooseAwsWhen: Strong mature platform and AWS infrastructure integration.
chooseAzureWhen: Strong enterprise governance and Microsoft data integration.
chooseGoogleCloudWhen: Strong unified AI platform, data integration and accelerator ecosystem.
complexityCaution: Advanced — Check team skill, existing notebooks, feature store needs and inference economics.

useWhenTrigger: Primary need is governed analytical SQL at scale
azureOption: Microsoft Fabric Warehouse / Azure Synapse Analytics
chooseAwsWhen: Strong AWS-native warehouse and S3 integration.
chooseAzureWhen: Strong Power BI/Fabric experience for Microsoft estates.
chooseGoogleCloudWhen: Strong serverless analytics and low-operations warehouse experience.
complexityCaution: Moderate — Compare concurrency, workload isolation, data sharing and cost model.

useWhenTrigger: Power BI, Microsoft 365 and Azure are already strategic
chooseAwsWhen: Use AWS when the data platform is already S3-centric.
chooseAzureWhen: Best fit for Power BI, Fabric and Microsoft governance.
chooseGoogleCloudWhen: Use GCP when BigQuery and AI are stronger drivers than Microsoft integration.
complexityCaution: Moderate — Avoid duplicating Fabric, Synapse and Databricks responsibilities without clear boundaries.

useWhenTrigger: Teams want serverless SQL and managed analytics
chooseAwsWhen: Good for S3 data-lake queries.
chooseAzureWhen: Good for Microsoft data and BI integration.
chooseGoogleCloudWhen: Strong serverless data warehouse and analytics operating model.
complexityCaution: Easy — Test representative queries and slot/consumption cost behaviour.

useWhenTrigger: Telemetry or events require low-latency processing and replay
chooseAwsWhen: Strong native streaming portfolio and AWS integration.
chooseAzureWhen: Strong Microsoft integration and event ingestion.
chooseGoogleCloudWhen: Strong managed streaming and unified batch/stream processing.
complexityCaution: Advanced — Define ordering, replay, retention, schema and exactly-once expectations.

useWhenTrigger: You genuinely require Kubernetes APIs, ecosystem and control
chooseAwsWhen: Strong AWS integration and broad infrastructure options.
chooseAzureWhen: Strong Microsoft identity, policy and hybrid integration.
chooseGoogleCloudWhen: Strong Kubernetes heritage and managed cluster experience.
complexityCaution: Advanced — Use a simpler container platform when Kubernetes-specific control is not required.

useWhenTrigger: Deploy HTTP or event-driven containers without cluster operations
chooseAwsWhen: Strong when ECS/Fargate and AWS services are already used.
chooseAzureWhen: Strong for Azure integration and Dapr-style microservices.
chooseGoogleCloudWhen: Strong simplicity, autoscaling and developer experience.
complexityCaution: Easy — Validate cold start, concurrency, networking and maximum execution constraints.

useWhenTrigger: Functions process events with low operational overhead
chooseAwsWhen: Strong event ecosystem, maturity and service integrations.
chooseAzureWhen: Strong Microsoft and Logic Apps integration.
chooseGoogleCloudWhen: Strong Cloud Run integration and developer simplicity.
complexityCaution: Easy — Do not use for long-running, stateful or highly specialised runtime needs.

scenario: Windows, Active Directory and SQL workload
useWhenTrigger: The application depends on Windows, Entra, SQL Server or Microsoft licensing
googleCloudOption: Compute Engine / Cloud SQL for SQL Server
chooseAwsWhen: Viable for AWS-standardised estates.
chooseAzureWhen: Default choice for Microsoft workload and licence integration.
chooseGoogleCloudWhen: Viable where GCP data/AI is strategic.
complexityCaution: Moderate — Assess licence mobility, support model and migration compatibility.

useWhenTrigger: The estate needs maximum infrastructure breadth and mature cloud-native building blocks
azureOption: Azure Virtual Machines and Azure portfolio
googleCloudOption: Compute Engine and Google Cloud portfolio
chooseAwsWhen: Strong breadth, infrastructure options and operational maturity.
chooseAzureWhen: Strong enterprise and Microsoft integration.
chooseGoogleCloudWhen: Strong network, data and cloud-native capabilities.
complexityCaution: Moderate — Existing contracts, skills and data gravity usually outweigh small feature differences.

useWhenTrigger: Servers, Kubernetes and data services span data centres and cloud
chooseAwsWhen: Strong for AWS-consistent on-premises infrastructure.
chooseAzureWhen: Strong unified enterprise hybrid management.
chooseGoogleCloudWhen: Strong Kubernetes-led multicloud and distributed cloud.
complexityCaution: Advanced — Define ownership, disconnected operation, identity and update responsibilities.

useWhenTrigger: Kubernetes fleets span multiple clouds
chooseAwsWhen: Good for AWS-centred EKS estates.
chooseAzureWhen: Good for Microsoft governance across clusters.
chooseGoogleCloudWhen: Strong Kubernetes-centred multicloud management.
complexityCaution: Advanced — Multicloud adds cost and complexity; require a clear business justification.

useWhenTrigger: A managed PostgreSQL-compatible operational database is required
chooseAwsWhen: Choose Aurora for AWS-native scale and integrations.
chooseAzureWhen: Choose Azure PostgreSQL for Microsoft estate integration.
chooseGoogleCloudWhen: Choose AlloyDB for high-performance PostgreSQL and Google data/AI integration.
complexityCaution: Moderate — Benchmark workload-specific latency, extensions, HA and migration compatibility.

useWhenTrigger: Strong consistency and horizontal/global scale are mandatory
azureOption: Azure SQL Hyperscale / Cosmos DB for PostgreSQL
chooseAwsWhen: Evaluate for AWS-native distributed SQL requirements.
chooseAzureWhen: Evaluate for Microsoft SQL or PostgreSQL distribution patterns.
chooseGoogleCloudWhen: Strong established globally distributed SQL architecture.
complexityCaution: Advanced — Do not choose distributed SQL when regional managed relational databases are sufficient.

useWhenTrigger: Access is primarily low-latency key-value at large scale
chooseAwsWhen: Strong serverless key-value database and AWS integration.
chooseAzureWhen: Strong multimodel and global distribution capabilities.
chooseGoogleCloudWhen: Choose Bigtable for wide-column scale or Firestore for document applications.
complexityCaution: Moderate — Start from access patterns, consistency and partition design—not brand preference.

useWhenTrigger: Queries depend on relationships, traversals and connected entities
chooseAwsWhen: Strong managed graph options in AWS.
chooseAzureWhen: Strong if Cosmos DB is already strategic.
chooseGoogleCloudWhen: Strong when graph and relational/analytics integration is desired.
complexityCaution: Advanced — Confirm graph model, query language, traversal depth and analytics requirements.

useWhenTrigger: Business users need governed dashboards and semantic models
chooseAwsWhen: Good AWS-native embedded and enterprise BI.
chooseAzureWhen: Strong enterprise adoption and Microsoft productivity integration.
chooseGoogleCloudWhen: Strong governed semantic modelling and Google data integration.
complexityCaution: Easy — BI tool choice should follow semantic model, user adoption and governance—not visuals alone.

useWhenTrigger: Workforce identity, SSO and enterprise access governance are central
chooseAwsWhen: Strong cloud-resource IAM and AWS federation.
chooseAzureWhen: Strong workforce identity and Microsoft enterprise ecosystem.
chooseGoogleCloudWhen: Strong Google Workspace and cloud IAM integration.
complexityCaution: Moderate — Separate workforce identity, customer identity and workload identity decisions.

useWhenTrigger: Security telemetry, detections, investigation and automated response are required
chooseAwsWhen: Strong security data foundation, often paired with partner SIEM.
chooseAzureWhen: Strong integrated Microsoft security operations ecosystem.
chooseGoogleCloudWhen: Strong cloud-scale security analytics and threat intelligence.
complexityCaution: Advanced — Assess telemetry sources, data retention, analyst workflows and ingestion economics.

useWhenTrigger: AI assets require inventory, policy, evaluation, safety and audit evidence
azureOption: Foundry Control Plane / Purview / Content Safety
googleCloudOption: Vertex AI governance / Model Armor / Dataplex
chooseAwsWhen: Strong AWS AI controls and security integration.
chooseAzureWhen: Strong enterprise governance, Purview and Microsoft security integration.
chooseGoogleCloudWhen: Strong model safety and data/AI platform integration.
complexityCaution: Advanced — Map controls to actual model, data, prompt, agent and human-oversight risks.

useWhenTrigger: APIs need policy, lifecycle, security, analytics and developer access
chooseAwsWhen: Strong AWS-native serverless API front door.
chooseAzureWhen: Strong enterprise API management and Microsoft integration.
chooseGoogleCloudWhen: Strong full-lifecycle API management with Apigee.
complexityCaution: Moderate — Distinguish lightweight API gateway needs from enterprise API programme needs.

useWhenTrigger: Services must be decoupled through queues, topics or events
chooseAwsWhen: Strong mature AWS event ecosystem.
chooseAzureWhen: Strong enterprise messaging and Microsoft integration.
chooseGoogleCloudWhen: Strong globally managed pub/sub and event routing.
complexityCaution: Easy — Choose queue, pub/sub and event bus based on delivery, ordering and routing semantics.

useWhenTrigger: Business applications require connectors, workflows and B2B integration
chooseAwsWhen: Strong AWS application and event integration.
chooseAzureWhen: Strong enterprise connectors, workflows and Microsoft integration.
chooseGoogleCloudWhen: Strong managed application integration and Google services.
complexityCaution: Moderate — Clarify whether the solution is integration, automation, API management or data movement.

useWhenTrigger: Teams need metrics, logs, traces, SLOs and application diagnostics
chooseAwsWhen: Strong AWS-native operations and service telemetry.
chooseAzureWhen: Strong Microsoft application and infrastructure monitoring.
chooseGoogleCloudWhen: Strong integrated cloud-native observability.
complexityCaution: Moderate — Standardise OpenTelemetry where portability and cross-platform visibility matter.

useWhenTrigger: Teams need source, build, artifacts, security and controlled deployment
googleCloudOption: Cloud Build / Cloud Deploy / Artifact Registry
chooseAwsWhen: Strong AWS-native deployment services.
chooseAzureWhen: Strong GitHub and enterprise developer ecosystem.
chooseGoogleCloudWhen: Strong cloud-native build and delivery experience.
complexityCaution: Moderate — Avoid duplicating toolchains; standardise golden paths and supply-chain controls.

useWhenTrigger: A migration programme needs discovery, waves, replication and modernisation
awsOption: Migration Hub / Application Migration Service / AWS Transform
googleCloudOption: Migration Center / Migrate to Virtual Machines
chooseAwsWhen: Strong migration portfolio and modernisation tooling.
chooseAzureWhen: Strong unified assessment and Microsoft estate migration.
chooseGoogleCloudWhen: Strong data and application modernisation for Google Cloud targets.
complexityCaution: Advanced — Select target architecture before selecting migration tooling.

useWhenTrigger: Devices and industrial assets require secure connectivity and edge processing
azureOption: Azure IoT Hub / IoT Operations / Digital Twins
googleCloudOption: Partner IoT platforms / Google Distributed Cloud
chooseAwsWhen: Strong broad first-party IoT portfolio.
chooseAzureWhen: Strong industrial, digital twin and Microsoft integration.
chooseGoogleCloudWhen: Use when Google data/AI and partner IoT ecosystem are strategic.
complexityCaution: Advanced — Google Cloud has no direct first-party replacement for the retired IoT Core.

useWhenTrigger: The workload needs live encoding, packaging, transport or ad insertion
googleCloudOption: Live Stream API / Transcoder / Media CDN
chooseAwsWhen: Strong comprehensive first-party media services.
chooseAzureWhen: Azure Media Services retirement means partner architecture is often required.
chooseGoogleCloudWhen: Strong selected video and delivery APIs.
complexityCaution: Advanced — Validate codec, latency, DRM, ad insertion and egress economics.

useWhenTrigger: Users require centrally managed Windows desktops or applications
chooseAwsWhen: Good cross-platform managed desktops.
chooseAzureWhen: Strong Windows, Entra and Microsoft 365 integration.
chooseGoogleCloudWhen: Usually partner-led for enterprise virtual desktop.
complexityCaution: Moderate — Include identity, profile, application packaging, network latency and licence costs.
